signal.headline: Hungary 6 days from pivotal vote as Tisza leads by 19–23 points; Benin election same day; US tariff defiance tests SCOTUS authority
signal.body: Hungary's April 12 parliamentary election is the most consequential single electoral event in the WDM's coverage geography this cycle. Independent polls give Tisza a 19–23 point lead among decided voters; leaked internal Fidesz data also projects Tisza victory. However, electoral redistricting requires a 3–6 point national advantage for a parliamentary majority, and Fidesz's 8 consecutive by-election wins signal rural constituency resilience not captured by national polling. On the same day, Benin's presidential election will confirm whether it crosses the Rapid Decay threshold — only ruling-bloc candidates are approved. In the United States, Trump's Section 122 global surcharge (10–15%) following the February 20 SCOTUS tariff ruling tests whether constitutional constraints on executive power hold under sustained pressure.

key_judgments.judgment: Hungary's April 12 election will be determinative: a Tisza parliamentary majority would represent the most significant democratic reversal of illiberal consolidation in EU history since 2010; a Fidesz retention despite record polling deficits would confirm that constituency gerrymandering and media capture can permanently insulate a governing party from popular will within EU institutional constraints.
key_judgments.source_basis: Medián (58/35%), 21 Kutatóközpont (56/37%), PolitPro aggregate (50.8/47.8%), CSIS scenario analysis, EUobserver institutional analysis, Polymarket (69% Tisza)

key_judgments.judgment: The US pattern of executive-SCOTUS confrontation has entered a new phase: after SCOTUS struck down IEEPA tariffs 6–3 (Feb 20), the administration immediately deployed Section 122 authority and continues to frame judicial review as illegitimate. This is not episodic — it is a sustained campaign to delegitimise the institutional constraint on executive power that the founding document places on tariff authority.
key_judgments.source_basis: SCOTUS ruling Learning Resources v. Trump (Feb 20), Section 122 proclamation, Reuters judicial interference analysis (Mar 7), Trump v. Barbara oral arguments (Apr 1)

key_judgments.judgment: Benin's April 12 presidential election will, with high probability, produce a ruling-bloc winner — confirming the threshold crossing from Watch List to Rapid Decay. The January 2026 parliamentary elections (109/109 seats to Talon-aligned parties) and the exclusion of credible opposition candidates from the presidential race are structural indicators, not episodic events.
key_judgments.trajectory: Declining — Rapid Decay threshold crossing expected April 12
key_judgments.source_basis: CIVICUS Watchlist March 2026, Wikipedia election documentation, WADR CENA timetable, Atlantic Council analysis

key_judgments.judgment: Georgia's institutional collapse is accelerating beyond the March 2026 law package: the three simultaneous laws (foreign grants criminalised, extremism law, 8-year civil society ban) represent the most concentrated legislative capture event in any EU candidate country since accession processes were formalised. There is no current pathway to reversal under the Kobakhidze government.
key_judgments.source_basis: FIDH March 2026, CIVICUS Watchlist, OSCE Moscow Mechanism (Mar 12), FCW intelligence-digest CMF-001/002

key_judgments.judgment: The authoritarian diffusion pattern documented by Freedom House (54 countries deteriorating vs 35 improving in 2025) and V-Dem (74% of world population in autocracies) is now structurally stable at its worst level since Cold War measurements began. The WDM's 15-country Rapid Decay roster and Watch List expansion to 13 countries reflects this global structural environment, not a measurement artefact.
key_judgments.source_basis: Freedom House Freedom in the World 2026, V-Dem Democracy Report 2026, V-Dem Dataset v16

Watch List threshold event — two ruling-bloc candidates only. If Wadagni wins, Benin crosses to Rapid Decay. Post-election crackdown on any protests would be the confirming event.

cross_monitor_flags.title: Hungary election FIMI saturation — three simultaneous confirmed operations
cross_monitor_flags.body: FCW intelligence-digest CMF-001 confirms three simultaneous confirmed/assessed Russian operations targeting Hungary April 12 election (SDA, Storm-1516, SVR Gamechanger) — highest documented pre-election FIMI density for a single EU election. WDM scores Hungary as Rapid Decay (5.0); the FIMI layer is the constitutive mechanism enabling Fidesz's information environment capture to persist even with record opposition poll leads. ESA dimension: if Orbán retains power with confirmed Russian FIMI support, this consolidates a Russian-aligned veto actor inside the EU for the next parliamentary cycle.

cross_monitor_flags.title: AI deepfakes active in US 2026 midterm campaigns — monitoring infrastructure vacuum
cross_monitor_flags.body: AGM cross-monitor flag cmf-002 confirmed: AI deepfakes now active in US midterm campaigns (Reuters, Mar 28). Simultaneously, Stanford Internet Observatory — the primary independent monitoring infrastructure for US election disinformation — was dismantled under political pressure. WDM tracks the electoral environment; AGM tracks the capability and governance vacuum; FCW tracks any state-attributed operations. The combination of operational AI deepfake deployment + monitoring vacuum + DNI omitting foreign interference from Worldwide Threats creates a compounding structural risk for November 2026.

cross_monitor_flags.title: US tariff-SCOTUS confrontation as rule of law and institutional resilience signal
cross_monitor_flags.body: SCOTUS tariff ruling (Feb 20) and Trump's immediate Section 122 response are simultaneously a GMM event (macro-financial) and a WDM event (rule of law, institutional resilience). GMM tracks the economic coercion signal; WDM tracks the executive-judicial constraint dynamic. The pattern of executive defiance: IEEPA struck down → Section 122 deployed same day → 97% of SCOTUS emergency requests frame judicial review as illegitimate → Trump attended SCOTUS oral arguments (Apr 1, first sitting president ever). This is not episodic; it is a sustained campaign.

cross_monitor_flags.title: Georgia EU candidate state institutional collapse — ESA/WDM convergence
cross_monitor_flags.body: Georgia's March 2026 three-law package (foreign grants criminalised, extremism law, 8-yr civil society ban) represents full institutional capture at the EU candidate state level. ESA tracks EU enlargement coherence and candidate state alignment; WDM tracks the democratic erosion. The Kobakhidze government's CPAC Hungary attendance (Mar 21) and alignment with Orbán's network confirms the cross-border authoritarian coordination signal tracked in WDM cms-002.

intelligence_items.headline: Final pre-election week: Tisza at 56–58% among decided voters; Fidesz at 35–37%; Polymarket 69% Tisza; but 8 consecutive by-election wins for Fidesz signal rural resilience
intelligence_items.summary: Medián (Apr 1): Tisza 58% vs Fidesz 35% among decided voters; 46/30 across wider electorate — largest documented gap. 21 Kutatóközpont (Apr 1): Tisza 56%, Fidesz 37% among likely voters. Polymarket: 69% Tisza. CSIS (Apr 4) scenarios: (1) Tisza simple majority; (2) disputed results with FIMI-driven delegitimisation; (3) Fidesz retention via redistricting; (4) constitutional supermajority for Tisza (27% probability). Leaked internal Fidesz data projects Tisza win. Counter-signal: Fidesz has won 8 consecutive by-elections since 2024 — zero Tisza entries. Rural constituency resilience not captured in national polling. Electoral system bonus: winner gets proportional seat amplification. Structural redistricting requires Tisza to exceed Fidesz by 3–6 points nationally for parliamentary majority.

intelligence_items.headline: SCOTUS tariff ruling → Section 122 same day; birthright citizenship arguments Apr 1; systematic SCOTUS delegitimisation campaign continuing
intelligence_items.summary: SCOTUS 6-3 struck down all IEEPA tariffs Feb 20 (Learning Resources v. Trump). Trump deployed Section 122 (10-15% global surcharge) within hours. Section 122 expires July 2026 unless Congress extends — Congress has passed resolutions against IEEPA tariffs. Trump v. Barbara oral arguments Apr 1 — SCOTUS will decide whether EO 14160 can redefine 14th Amendment by executive action. Trump became first sitting president to attend SCOTUS oral arguments (Apr 1). Reuters (Mar 7): 97% of Trump's 31 emergency SCOTUS requests argue judicial interference — systematic delegitimisation of judicial review. DNI Gabbard omitted foreign election interference from Mar 19 Worldwide Threats hearing — first time in nearly a decade.

intelligence_items.headline: April 12 presidential election — two ruling-bloc candidates, all 109 assembly seats held by Talon-aligned parties, opposition excluded
intelligence_items.summary: January 11 2026 elections: all 109 National Assembly seats won by parties supporting President Talon (UPR 60, BR 49). Turnout 36.73%. Opposition LD party alleged vote buying and ballot stuffing. April 12 presidential election: only Wadagni (Finance Minister, ruling-bloc) and Hounkpè (minor ruling-bloc candidate) cleared. CENA barred all credible opposition candidates. CIVICUS March Watchlist: 'Obstructed' civic space, journalists arrested, media suspended, protests routinely banned. If Wadagni wins with no credible opposition, Benin crosses the WDM Rapid Decay threshold — Watch List entry will be upgraded.

intelligence_items.headline: March 2026 three-law package fully in force — FCW FIMI dimension confirmed
intelligence_items.summary: Georgia's March 2026 legislative package (foreign grants criminalised with 6-yr sentence, 'extremism against constitutional order' law, 8-yr civil society ban for professionals) is the most concentrated single-session legislative capture event in any EU candidate country. FIDH called for immediate repeal. Nine-party opposition alliance formed — politically significant, operationally constrained by the new extremism law. FCW CMF-002 confirmed: if Orbán retains power with Russian FIMI support, Georgia-Hungary alignment creates a Russian-adjacent bloc inside and adjacent to EU institutions. Kobakhidze-CPAC Hungary (Mar 21) documents operational coordination between these two governments.

intelligence_items.headline: 621 confirmed releases as of March 8 — 500+ still held; revolving-door arrests continuing; transition fragile
intelligence_items.summary: Foro Penal (as of 8 March): 621 confirmed releases since Jan 8 announcement — up from 448 on Feb 20. However, of 1,557 applicants under the amnesty law (Feb 19), only 91 confirmed released under the law itself by Feb 24. UN Fact-Finding Mission: 87 new politically motivated arrests since Jan 3. Transition pattern: real but coerced and transactional. Trump administration prioritising oil access over democratic conditions — removing structural pressure for genuine reform. Recovery classification provisional only.

intelligence_items.headline: Freedom House 2026: 20th consecutive year of global decline; V-Dem: 74% of world population in autocracies — first time autocracies outnumber democracies since Cold War
intelligence_items.summary: Freedom House Freedom in the World 2026 (covering 2025 data): 54 countries deteriorated, 35 improved — largest net negative in 20 years. V-Dem Democracy Report 2026: 74% of world population in autocracies (6 billion people); only 7% in liberal democracies (600 million). Number of liberal democracies: 31 (down from 45 in 2009). Countries in active autocratisation episodes: 44 (record high). Authoritarian regimes increasingly collaborating to undermine civil society, international institutions, and election monitoring. The WDM's 15-country Rapid Decay roster reflects this global structural environment.

signal_panel.headline: Hungary April 12 — the institutional democracy test of the decade
signal_panel.body: Six days from Hungary's parliamentary election, the most significant test of whether democratic transitions can occur within a captured electoral system. Independent polls give Tisza a 19–23 point lead among decided voters; Polymarket prices a Tisza win at 69%. But Fidesz has won every by-election since 2024 (8 consecutive), and the redistricted system requires Tisza to outperform by 3–6 points nationally for a majority. Three simultaneous confirmed Russian FIMI operations (SDA, Storm-1516, SVR Gamechanger) are targeting the election — the highest documented pre-election FIMI density for any EU election. CSIS identifies four scenarios, including disputed results and delegitimisation campaigns. On the same day, Benin's presidential election is expected to confirm its crossing of the Rapid Decay threshold — with only ruling-bloc candidates approved. In the United States, the pattern of executive defiance of SCOTUS constraints continues: tariffs struck down on Feb 20, Section 122 deployed same day, and Trump attended SCOTUS oral arguments April 1 (a presidential first) as the court considered birthright citizenship reinterpretation by executive order.

weekly_brief:
Global democratic health enters its most consequential week of the 2026 cycle. The World Democracy Monitor carries 15 countries in Rapid Decay, 5 in Recovery, and 13 on the Watch List — the highest combined monitoring load since the monitor's launch. Two threshold events converge on April 12.

**Hungary: the decade's defining electoral test.** Six days from the parliamentary election, independent polling gives Péter Magyar's Tisza party a 19–23 point lead among decided voters (Medián: 58/35%; 21 Kutatóközpont: 56/37%; Polymarket: 69% Tisza). Leaked internal Fidesz data also projects a Tisza victory. The critical structural constraint remains: Hungary's redistricted mixed electoral system (106 single-member districts, 93 proportional seats) requires Tisza to outperform Fidesz by at least 3–6 percentage points nationally to achieve a parliamentary majority. Fidesz has won all 8 by-elections since the Tisza party emerged in 2024 — zero Tisza entries — demonstrating rural constituency resilience that national polling does not fully capture. Three simultaneous confirmed Russian information operations (SDA, Storm-1516, SVR Gamechanger) are targeting the election — the highest documented FIMI density for any single EU election (FCW intelligence-digest). CSIS (April 4) identifies four post-election scenarios, including disputed results with FIMI-driven delegitimisation. The WDM assesses: a Tisza majority would represent the most significant institutional democratic reversal within the EU since Hungary's illiberal turn began in 2010; a Fidesz retention despite record polling deficits would confirm that captured media environments and redistricted electoral architectures can permanently insulate incumbents from popular will within EU institutions. Hungary remains Rapid Decay (5.0↗) — score holds pending April 12 outcome.

**Benin: Watch List threshold crossing expected April 12.** On the same day as Hungary's election, Benin holds its presidential vote with only two candidates — both from the ruling bloc. Following January's parliamentary elections where all 109 National Assembly seats went to Talon-aligned parties (turnout 36.73%), CENA excluded all credible opposition candidates from the presidential race. CIVICUS March 2026 Watchlist rates Benin as 'Obstructed' — journalists arrested, media suspended, protests routinely banned. If Wadagni wins (expected), Benin crosses the WDM Rapid Decay threshold; any post-election crackdown on civil society would be the confirming structural signal. A Watch List → Rapid Decay upgrade will be processed in Issue 5.

**United States: judicial constraint under sustained pressure.** The executive-SCOTUS dynamic has entered a qualitatively new phase. After the Court's 6–3 ruling (Feb 20) struck down all IEEPA tariffs, the administration deployed Section 122 authority within hours (10–15% global surcharge), effectively replacing one emergency power with another. Trump became the first sitting president to attend oral arguments at SCOTUS (April 1) during the Trump v. Barbara case testing whether the 14th Amendment birthright citizenship clause can be reinterpreted by executive order — decision expected June/July. Reuters (March 7): 97% of Trump's 31 emergency SCOTUS requests frame judicial review as interference with presidential power — a systematic delegitimisation campaign. DNI Gabbard omitted foreign election interference from the Worldwide Threats hearing (March 19) — the first such omission in nearly a decade, structurally significant for the November 2026 midterms. US remains Rapid Decay (5.5↑).

**Georgia: legislative capture complete.** The March 2026 three-law package — criminalising foreign grants (6-yr sentences), an 'extremism against constitutional order' law, and an 8-year civil society ban for political participation — is fully in force. This is the most concentrated single-session legislative capture in any EU candidate country. FIDH has called for repeal; nine opposition parties have formed an alliance but are operationally constrained by the new extremism law itself. FCW confirmed that Georgia's Kobakhidze government is operationally coordinated with Hungary's Orbán government through the CPAC Hungary network (March 21). Georgia remains Rapid Decay (8.5↑).

**Global context.** Freedom House's Freedom in the World 2026 documents the 20th consecutive year of global democratic decline: 54 countries deteriorated vs 35 improved in 2025. V-Dem confirms autocracies now outnumber liberal democracies for the first time since the Cold War (92 vs 31), with 74% of the world's population living under autocratic systems. This is the structural environment in which the WDM's 15-country Rapid Decay roster operates.

institutional_integrity_flags.headline: DNI Gabbard's 2026 Worldwide Threats Hearing omitted foreign election interference — first time in nearly a decade
institutional_integrity_flags.summary: DNI Tulsi Gabbard's March 19 hearing omitted all foreign election interference threats for the first time in nearly a decade. Defense One (Mar 18): structural signal that the intelligence apparatus designed to protect electoral integrity has been redirected. Reuters (Mar 19): top US spy cited no foreign threats to November 2026 elections.

institutional_integrity_flags.headline: 97% of Trump SCOTUS emergency requests argue judicial interference; Trump attends oral arguments (first sitting president) Apr 1
institutional_integrity_flags.summary: Reuters (Mar 7): 97% of Trump's 31 Supreme Court emergency requests argue judicial interference — systematic campaign to reframe judicial review as illegitimate. Trump attended SCOTUS oral arguments Apr 1 (Trump v. Barbara, birthright citizenship) — unprecedented for a sitting president. SCOTUS has now produced two significant constraint rulings (IEEPA tariffs Feb 20; Pentagon press credentialing Mar 20). V-Dem: rule of law the most common specific indicator declining globally.

institutional_integrity_flags.headline: Redistricted electoral system creates structural gap between popular will and parliamentary seats — FIMI layer provides information environment advantage
institutional_integrity_flags.summary: Hungary's mixed electoral system (redistricted 2011) requires Tisza to outperform Fidesz by 3–6 percentage points nationally to achieve parliamentary majority. Fidesz's media capture means state and aligned media reaches 80%+ of rural voters — Tisza reaches primarily urban audiences. FCW: 3 simultaneous Russian operations targeting the election. Combined effect: a system where a party trailing by 19–23 points in independent polling may retain power.

institutional_integrity_flags.headline: March 2026 three-law package — most concentrated legislative capture at EU candidate state level
institutional_integrity_flags.summary: Three laws passed in a single session: foreign grants criminalised (6-yr sentence), 'extremism against constitutional order' covering public statements, 8-yr ban on civil society professionals from political participation. FIDH: no pathway to reversal under Kobakhidze government. EU enlargement mechanism cannot currently impose consequences — Rule of Law mechanism applies only to member states, not candidates.

mimicry_chain_update.note: Russian Foreign Agent Law chain, Foreign Agent Tax chain, Emergency Detention chain — all carrying from Issue 3. No new links added this issue. Full chain data in persistent-state.json.
